mapping.a_element: self-duality / anti-self-duality of 2-forms (Hodge involution on differential forms)
mapping.b_element: particle-hole symmetry ("duality") protecting sectors at half-filling

mapping.a_element: SO(3) gauge symmetry and its removal by polar decomposition / index theorem
mapping.b_element: discrete point-group symmetry and Mulliken classification of eigenstates

brief_justification: The overlap is only lexical ("self-dual"/"duality" and "symmetry"): paper A solves a nonlinear elliptic Yang-Mills PDE on a 4-manifold using the Hodge self-dual/anti-self-dual splitting of 2-forms plus gauge-fixing and a Fredholm index theorem, whereas paper B diagonalizes a finite-dimensional tight-binding Hubbard Hamiltonian and classifies eigenstates by point-group representations with particle-hole duality. Continuous gauge-theoretic PDE machinery versus discrete quantum spectral/representation-theory machinery do not correspond role-for-role, so no shared underlying method holds.
